---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")

  # Variables
  status_var: "tech_adopted"

  # Optional: fixed category order as comma-separated values (e.g. "Green,Yellow,Red").
  # Leave "" to order by frequency (largest first).
  category_order: ""

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Hardware Rollout Adoption"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Composition Overview: Waffle Chart"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5.5,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers and values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# Handles ANY number of categories (binary, ternary triage, or more).
# ==============================================================================
# 1. Count each category of the status variable
cat_counts <- raw_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(.data[[params$status_var]]),
         nzchar(as.character(.data[[params$status_var]]))) %>%
  count(Category = as.character(.data[[params$status_var]]), name = "n")

if (nrow(cat_counts) == 0) stop("No non-missing values found in '", params$status_var, "'.")
if (nrow(cat_counts) > 8) stop(
  "'", params$status_var, "' has ", nrow(cat_counts),
  " distinct categories — too many for a readable waffle chart. ",
  "Choose a variable with 8 or fewer categories."
)

# 2. Resolve category order: YAML-specified, else by frequency
if (nzchar(params$category_order)) {
  wanted <- str_squish(str_split(params$category_order, ",")[[1]])
  lvls <- c(intersect(wanted, cat_counts$Category),
            setdiff(cat_counts$Category, wanted))
} else {
  lvls <- cat_counts %>% arrange(desc(n)) %>% pull(Category)
}

# 3. Exact percentages + largest-remainder rounding so squares sum to exactly 100
cat_counts <- cat_counts %>%
  mutate(Category = factor(Category, levels = lvls)) %>%
  arrange(Category) %>%
  mutate(pct_exact = 100 * n / sum(n))

squares <- floor(cat_counts$pct_exact)
leftover <- 100 - sum(squares)
if (leftover > 0) {
  top_up <- order(cat_counts$pct_exact - squares, decreasing = TRUE)[seq_len(leftover)]
  squares[top_up] <- squares[top_up] + 1
}
cat_counts$squares <- squares

# 4. Legend labels carry the exact share, so the chart needs no axis at all
cat_counts <- cat_counts %>%
  mutate(Label = paste0(Category, " — ", round(pct_exact), "%"))

# 5. Build the 10x10 grid, filled bottom-to-top, left-to-right
waffle_data <- expand.grid(x = 1:10, y = 1:10) %>%
  arrange(y, x) %>%
  mutate(Label = factor(
    rep(cat_counts$Label, times = cat_counts$squares),
    levels = cat_counts$Label
  ))

# 6. Colors: semantic if the categories carry universal meaning
#    (Green/Yellow/Red triage, Yes/No, risk levels), otherwise house palette
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)
semantic <- cerp_match_semantic(as.character(cat_counts$Category))
waffle_colors <- setNames(
  if (!is.null(semantic)) unname(semantic) else cerp_fill_n(nrow(cat_counts)),
  cat_counts$Label
)

# 7. Headline for the subtitle: lead with the largest category
top_cat <- cat_counts %>% slice_max(pct_exact, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
```


```{r plot-waffle}
ggplot(waffle_data, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = Label)) +

  # Squares with a background-colored border for the classic waffle separation
  geom_tile(color = cerp_cols[["bg"]], linewidth = 2) +

  # Lock the aspect ratio so squares stay square
  coord_equal() +

  scale_fill_manual(values = waffle_colors) +

  theme_cerp() +

  # Strip all axes and grids — the legend percentages tell the whole story
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "left"
  ) +

  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (params$metric_name),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else (paste0( "Each square is 1% of the sample (N = ", scales::comma(sum(cat_counts$n)), "). ", top_cat$Category, " accounts for ", round(top_cat$pct_exact), "%." )),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
